parameters.query:
INSERT INTO draft_log (draftId, topic, audience, logged_at)

parameters.content:
### Receive Inbound Webhook

Entry point: listens for POST calls on `/wf05607-draft`. Use the TEST url while building, switch callers to the PRODUCTION url after publishing.

Next: Stamp & Shape Draft

### Stamp & Shape Draft

Shapes the item: writes `draftId`, `receivedAt`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Payload Valid?

### Payload Valid?

Two-way gate on `={{ $json.draftId }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Detect Sentiment · ②→ Record Rejected Input

### Record Rejected Input

Appends a new row to 'tracking sheet' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Detect Sentiment

Scores the text's sentiment; ports fire positive / neutral / negative.

Ports: ①→ Escalate Negative — Slack · ②→ Log Neutral Feedback, Capture Failure Context · ③→ Thank the Sender

⚠ failures exit via the error rail

### OpenRouter Chat Model

Chat model powering the AI step (model: `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.5`). Swap providers here without rewiring.

Feeds **Detect Sentiment** as `languageModel`

🔑 OpenRouter API key

### Escalate Negative — Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#content`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

Ports: ①→ Collect All Outcomes · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Log Neutral Feedback

Appends a new row to 'Draft Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Collect All Outcomes · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Thank the Sender

Posts to a Discord channel via an incoming webhook — no bot token needed, the webhook URL on the credential is the only secret.

Ports: ①→ Collect All Outcomes · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Discord webhook URL

### Collect All Outcomes

Rejoins 3 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Stamp Completion Time

### Stamp Completion Time

Stamps `completedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Persist Run Metadata

### Persist Run Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Load Previous Snapshot

### Load Previous Snapshot

Runs a parameterized SQL statement against Postgres — values are bound via `queryReplacement`, not string-interpolated, so it's immune to SQL injection.

Ports: ①→ Compute Drift vs Snapshot · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Postgres connection

### Compute Drift vs Snapshot

Custom JavaScript: Compare current metrics with the previous snapshot

Next: Fingerprint for Idempotency

### Fingerprint for Idempotency

Custom JavaScript: Build a stable idempotency key per draft (djb2 hash, no deps)

Next: Drop Already-Processed Items

### Drop Already-Processed Items

Cross-execution dedupe: remembers keys it has processed before and drops repeats — the idempotency guard.

Next: Enrich with Reference Data

### Enrich with Reference Data

GET https://enrichment.example.com/v2/lookup — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Ports: ①→ Fold In Enrichment Fields · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Fold In Enrichment Fields

Shapes the item: writes `enrichedAt`, `enrichmentSource`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Serialize Run Output (CSV)

### Serialize Run Output (CSV)

Serializes items to a CSV binary file.

Next: Archive Row Snapshot

### Archive Row Snapshot

Appends a new row to 'Draft Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Offload to Post-Process Sub-workflow · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Offload to Post-Process Sub-workflow

Delegates to a reusable sub-workflow (single-responsibility building block) and waits for its result.

Ports: ①→ Record Sub-workflow Result · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail

### Record Sub-workflow Result

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Sort Drafts Newest First

### Sort Drafts Newest First

Orders items (newest first) so limits and digests pick the most relevant records.

Next: Stamp Processing Time

### Stamp Processing Time

Stamps `processedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Cap Batch Volume

### Cap Batch Volume

Caps the stream at 100 items to protect downstream quotas.

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

### Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

Appends a new row to 'Draft Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Slack · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Send Summary to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#content`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#content`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

Next: Park in Dead-Letter Queue

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Park in Dead-Letter Queue

Runs a parameterized SQL statement against Postgres — values are bound via `queryReplacement`, not string-interpolated, so it's immune to SQL injection.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Postgres connection

# Digest meeting notes with PostgreSQL and Discord hands-free

**Pattern:** AI Summarize & Route · **Level:** Architect · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Digest meeting notes with PostgreSQL and Discord hands-free. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual newsletters is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Receive Inbound Webhook → Stamp & Shape Draft → Payload Valid? → Detect Sentiment → Escalate Negative — Slack → Collect All Outcomes → Stamp Completion Time → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to ai summarize & route without manual effort
- Anyone automating newsletters in content creation
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Has retries and error handling built in
- Responds in seconds
- Runs 24/7 in the background

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Discord webhook URL; Google OAuth2 / service account; OpenRouter API key; Postgres connection; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

A three-way branch on sentiment, not a single IF, because 'neutral' deserves different handling than 'negative' — quietly logging positive feedback while paging a human only for genuine complaints keeps the alert channel's signal-to-noise ratio high for whoever is on the other end.

## Practice exercises

About as deep as this pattern goes — extend it:

1. Add a logging step to the neutral branch too and compare its volume against the negative branch.
2. Test borderline phrases through the sentiment node to see where the positive/neutral line actually falls.
3. Add a 4th branch for a specific emotion if your sentiment provider supports finer-grained labels.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Failures are also parked in a dead-letter queue for replay. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🔐 Security

Never trust inbound payloads: the webhook path is unguessable. Add header auth or IP allow-listing for defense in depth. Secrets live in n8n credentials — never in node parameters.

## 🤖 AI cluster

Purple nodes form one AI unit: the root node orchestrates, sub-nodes plug in via typed ports (model / memory / tools / parser). Swap the chat model without rewiring anything else. Costs scale with tokens — keep prompts lean and cap iterations.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your newsletters process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
